title: Dryopsis crassirachis
intro: Dryopsis crassirachis is a fern species, native to East Asia and parts of the Pacific Northwest in North America, known for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Dryopsis crassirachis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional Chinese medicine.
